items.title: Book tells story of Timorese asylum seekers' daring escape to Darwin

children.content: Jose da Costa's escape from Indonesian-occupied East Timor in 1995 has been detailed in a new book launched at the Northern Territory Writers Festival this weekend.

gallery.altText: A Timorese man, smiling, black curly short hair, light olive polo shirt, coastal background.

thumbnail.altText: A Timorese man, smiling, black curly short hair, light olive polo shirt, coastal background.

items.title: Aboriginal families in Federal Court bid for traditional ownership recognition

children.content: Two groups of Indigenous families have claimed in the Federal Court they should be recognised as the traditional owners of the NT's Cobourg Peninsula.

image.altText: The coastline of a remote beach.

items.title: This herb could halt a major NT gas project

children.content: The NT government is arguing its planned mega Middle Arm gas industry precinct will be needed to help shore up Australia's future fuel and energy supplies — but it has just identified that a tiny endangered herb could be growing in the way.

image.altText: A small herb plant, green with three leaves growing out of a stone / pebble ground area.

items.title: Leaked data shows NT diphtheria cases surged a month before alert was issued

children.content: Internal Northern Territory Health data leaked to the ABC reveals diphtheria case numbers surged in the NT a month before a public health alert was issued. The data also offers the first public insight into the regions hit hardest by the outbreak.

gallery.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

thumbnail.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

items.title: Indonesian importer buys up big at NT's biggest bull sale

children.content: The Northern Territory's biggest bull sale has recorded one of its strongest results, despite high fuel prices and a big wet season.

image.altText: Two brahman bulls, white with a hump neck in view, only one of their heads visible, people in the background holding paper.

items.title: Refugee who tried to flee the Taliban 15 times finally safe to play cricket

children.content: After the Taliban banned women's sport in Afghanistan, most of the women's cricket team ended up relocating to Australia. Now, they're on tour in the Northern Territory — "a happy ending" after a traumatic journey.

image.altText: Three women in view, one in centre mid-speech, wearing a pink jersey, black hijab, all standing on a green sports field.

items.title: The spy network that fed Castro intel ahead of plane downing at heart of murder charges

children.content: In the indictment of former Cuban president Raúl Castro, prosecutors alluded to a network of spies called The Wasp Network. Here is what happened when they were deployed to Florida in the 1990s.

gallery.altText: A woman talks on a payphone attached to a wall where a notice about the Cuban 5 has been painted
A small, white dual-tail plane flying over dark blue ocean water and boats.
A mural of the Cuban 5 outside a building.

thumbnail.altText: A woman talks on a payphone attached to a wall where a notice about the Cuban 5 has been painted
A small, white dual-tail plane flying over dark blue ocean water and boats.
A mural of the Cuban 5 outside a building.

items.title: Penrith rests Origin stars for Warriors blockbuster, Raiders roll Cowboys

children.content: Nathan Cleary, Isaah Yeo and Brian To'o are all rested for Penrith's top-of-the-table clash with the Warriors, after the Raiders down the Cowboys in Canberra. Follow live.

image.altText: Two rugby league players in black sit on the bench

items.title: GWS notches back-to-back wins with big victory over Dees

children.content: The Giants back up last week's trouncing of Brisbane with a professional 49-point victory over Melbourne in Alice Springs. Follow live.

image.altText: A group of AFL players in orange high five and celebrate a goal in afternoon sun

items.title: Another US air strike in the Pacific kills 'narco-terrorists' taking death toll to 202

children.content: It is the latest strike in a months-long series of attacks from the US on suspected drug trafficking boats in the Caribbean Sea and eastern Pacific Ocean.

image.altText: An aerial view of an explosion at sea

articles.title: US fires missile into ship trying to breach blockade to reach Iran

children.content: The US military fires a missile into the engine room of the merchant vessel trying to break through its blockade of Iranian ports, the Central Command says.

image.altText: A photo of the Lian Star merchant vessel at sea.

articles.title: Trump to headline 250th anniversary fair after musicians cancel

children.content: Martina McBride, Bret Michaels, Young MC, Morris Day and The Time and The Commodores all said they would not perform after alleging they were misled about the nature of the event.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaks during a rally, his mouth is open. He is wearing a navy blue suit and red tie

gallery.altText: An aerial view of an explosion at sea

thumbnail.altText: An aerial view of an explosion at sea

items.title: Budget won't be 'main driver' if house prices fall, housing minister says

children.content: The housing minister says the federal budget will have only a "mild" impact on house prices, and if predictions bear out that house prices may drop by 10 per cent, that won't be due to the government's tax changes.

image.altText: a female politician wearing a white blazer talking

items.title: Police urge community caution as man remains at large after Melbourne double stabbing

children.content: Victoria Police are urging people to be cautious after two stabbing incidents across Melbourne, including one where a car was driven into a home in the city's east.

image.altText: A car which has crashed into a house.

items.title: The combo that makes tonight's full moon a 'last in a generation' occurrence

children.content: If you look up to the full moon at sunset this evening, you may notice it looks ever so slightly smaller than usual.

thumbnail.altText: flying fox silhouetted against a full moon

items.title: Police beat a woman in a Sydney street. Their own camera captured their cruelty

children.content: The family of a woman brutally assaulted by police in Sydney has fought to have body-worn camera vision released, in the hope officers will be better trained in responding to acute mental health situations.

image.altText: A still from a body-worn camera as a plain clothes officer draws his leg back to kick a naked woman on a street.

items.title: Perth and WA's south brace for 'rarely experienced' storm

children.content: With a strength only seen every three to five years, the Bureau of Meteorology warns the deep low pressure system is likely to produce destructive wind gusts from Lancelin to Albany, including Perth.

image.altText: A cafe surrounded by water

items.title: Love story: Reconnecting with the 'one that got away'

children.content: For a long time, I considered my teenage crush the one that got away. That all changed when we reconnected two decades later.

gallery.altText: Shannon, Carlie and their daughter Rowan

thumbnail.altText: Shannon, Carlie and their daughter Rowan

items.title: After five days in a dark cave, a scientist emerged with life-changing insights

children.content: Spending big money to do nothing in the dark is on the rise. Scientist Kiana Aran chronicled what happened to her when she tried it.

image.altText: headshot of Kiana wearing a black eye mask, there is a small round, black gadget on her forehead.

items.title: Former teachers' pets, rejoice. Guy Montgomery's spelling quiz is back

children.content: Welcome back to your Sunday morning quiz with everyone's favourite word wonderboy.

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: Why are Australians still getting lost and dying in the outback?

children.content: Kelly’s son and ex-husband died "stupid" deaths in the Australian outback. She’s on a mission to make sure no more lives are lost.

gallery.altText: A white car driving in the middle of the bush.

thumbnail.altText: A white car driving in the middle of the bush.

items.title: Socceroos defeated by Mexico despite encouraging second-half showing

children.content: The Socceroos are beaten 1-0 by Mexico in their penultimate World Cup warm up match despite an improved and impressive second-half performance at the Rose Bowl.

image.altText: Mo Toure looks sad during a game for the Socceroos.

items.title: Dozens of arrests during Champions League celebrations on Paris streets

children.content: Celebrations of Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League victory have turned violent, as fans clashed with police on the streets of the French capital.

image.altText: Police are silhouetted as a firework explodes on a Paris street.

items.title: As One Nation broadens its economic appeal, the Liberals return to culture wars

children.content: The Coalition's answer to the rise of conservative populism has been to treat it primarily as a cultural phenomenon. Under Angus Taylor, that instinct appears to be hardening.

image.altText: An older man wearing a suit gives an impassioned speech.

items.title: Smoke engulfed their cities. Did it make their children sick?

children.content: Years after bushfires covered their cities in smoke, mothers are left wondering if their children's chronic illnesses began with what was in the air before they were born.

image.altText: Illustration of an ultrasound image of a fetus before a window with bushfires burning in the background.

items.title: Team 'astonished' as rider kicked out of Giro d'Italia Women

children.content: Lorena Wiebes powered to victory on stage one of Giro d'Italia Women, but was later kicked off the race for having an underweight bike, a finding that shocked her team.

items.title: Flotilla activists take evidence of alleged abuse to international court

children.content: Eleven Australians detained by Israeli forces while attempting to deliver aid to Gaza have submitted evidence to the International Criminal Court alleging abuse, torture and other violations during their detention.

image.altText: three australian activists dressed in grey clothes in instanbul airport

items.title: Police 'open' to learning from volunteers who found remains in renewed search

children.content: A renewed search by volunteers for missing Tasmanian man Peter Willoughby led to the discovery of remains on Saturday. It's the second time this year a group of volunteers has made a significant find during the search for a missing person.

image.altText: A group of people wearing high visibility clothing search in a line, walking through dry eucalypt forest in the mist.

items.title: Is the government really overhauling employment services?

children.content: The government isn't rebuilding the Commonwealth Employment Service, but will its proposed changes fix a broken system?

image.altText: Rishworth speaks at the NPC lectern

items.title: At 15, Mohamed Semra made headlines. At 27, he’s making history

children.content: At just 27, Mohamed Semra is not your average mayor. He overcame a childhood stutter and a viral moment in a Melbourne Apple Store to make political history – and he is just getting started.

image.altText: Mohamed Semra wears black mayoral robes and chain while seated at solid timber chambers desk, his hands clasped

items.title: Rare images capture pioneer life on WA's south coast in the early 1900s

children.content: Pioneering photographer Bert Saw captured Federation-era life and experiences in Denmark, Albany and surrounding communities throughout the 1900s.

Bert photo of settlers on boat

items.title: In a dark courtroom, a video of horrific accidents plays as a woman cries

children.content: From hoons to drink drivers to people driving without a licence, one magistrate is trying to make sure the people brought before her know how quickly things can turn deadly on the roads.

image.altText: court sketch showing man in blue polo in front of a magistrate

items.title: 'Losing a generation': Why some countries are moving fast on AI in schools

children.content: Countries in Asia are emerging as the world's early adopters of AI in education, as the global race to lead in the technology accelerates.

image.altText: Two young boys in school uniform looking at a laptop screen together.

items.title: AUKUS partners unveil plan to develop underwater drones

children.content: The US, Australia and United Kingdom have unveiled a new "signature" project to develop cutting edge weapons systems and sensors for underwater drones as they try to reinvigorate the second pillar of the AUKUS agreement.

image.altText: Three men in suits stand for a press conference in front of the US, Australian and British flags

items.title: This preacher was once part of NZ's gang world. Now he's trying to heal it

children.content: In a country where there now more gang members than police officers, a former gang member turned Christian pastor is trying to turn things around for others by addressing the root causes of gang life.

image.altText: A heavily tattooed man with a traditional Māori facial tattoos sits on a motorbike, wears a vest with "My Brothers Keeper" patch

items.title: Gaming an avenue for remote social connections outside sport

children.content: While sport is the lifeblood of some country towns, alternatives abound for those looking to build connections elsewhere.
